DISCOVERY 612, NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSE TO INTERROGATORIES AND RESPONSE TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION AND INSPECTION OF TANGIBLE THINGS IS MOOT AS RESPONSES HAVE BEEN SERVED BY MARISOL TORRES AND JESUS JIMENEZ. DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSE TO INTERROGATORIES AND RESPONSE TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION AND INSPECTION OF TANGIBLE THINGS IS GRANTED AS TO PLAINTIFF CERVANDO ORENDANO. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE LATE RESPONSES AND THE FAILURE TO RESPOND WERE A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. PLAINTIFF CERVANDO ORENDANO IS ORDERED TO RESPOND WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED AGAINST PLAINTIFFS, JOINTLY AND SEVERALLY, IN THE AMOUNT OF $306.30, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. (612/HEWLETT)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-08-2005 Off Calendar

Minor'S Compromise In Scd.
Off Calendar May-19-2005 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Minor's Compromise hearing this day.
Dept. 218, Comm. Sue Kaplan, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: Maria Viscio.
John Hanlin, Esq. for Petitioner: Marisol Torres, present as Guardian at litem for: Jesus Jimenez, a minor Claimant, and with Spanish Interpreter: Jose Granados.
Petitioner and Interpreter, were sworn and examined.
On the basis of evidence received, the Petition is granted and compromise is approved.
Attorney's Fees $625.00
Medical Expenses $704.96
A net balance of $1,329.96 to be deposited into a blocked account with Bank of America, Powell & 5th Street, San Francisco, CA., no withdrawals shall be made without a written court order or until the minor reaches the age of 18 years.
Proposed Orders received in court and will be signed by Comm. Kaplan.
4:00 P.M.
Order Approving Compromise of Claim and Order to Deposit Money Into Blocked Account signed by Comm. Kaplan.
